uses: Vernonia lewisii is primarily found in the southwestern United States and Mexico. benefits: Vernonia lewisii, also known as Lewis' Ironweed, has been found to possess several health benefits. intro: Vernonia lewisii, also known as Lewis's ironweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and improve overall cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Vernonia lewisii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its purported health benefits, and is also used in some herbal supplements and topical creams.
